audit_items.proof_trace: All SHA1 functions lack internal NULL guards (standard for C SHA1 implementations), but every LDAP-reachable caller in passwd.c passes stack-allocated context structs (&SHA1context) that are never NULL, and data pointers come from BER-decoded bervals whose bv_val is always allocated when bv_len>0. The one outbound pointer risk (digest in lutil_SHA1Final) is guarded by an explicit if(digest) check at L196. The convenience functions lutil_SHA1End/File/Data are not called from the LDAP authentication path.
